skill.name: localhero
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through Localhero.ai i18n workflows, including source string edits, glossary use, project settings, and translation generation.
skill.summary: Manage Localhero.ai source strings and translation workflows in supported coding assistants.
skill.author: localheroai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution findings, but most are markdown code fences or inline backticks documenting Localhero CLI usage, not Ruby execution. Real risk remains because the skill authorizes an external npx CLI that can contact Localhero.ai, modify translation files, and use API keys. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External Localhero CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash commands matching npx @localheroai/cli * and instructs agents to run translation, glossary, and settings commands. This is legitimate for an i18n workflow, but it can execute an external npm CLI that reads and writes project translation files and may contact a remote service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and workflow commands directly confirm external CLI execution. The intent appears legitimate, so this is elevated operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Remote Translation Service Interaction
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to use Localhero.ai for translation management and points users to the web UI and CLI package. Project source strings, glossary data, settings, and translations may be sent to or retrieved from the service during normal use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly describe Localhero.ai network-backed translation management. No evidence shows unauthorized exfiltration, but users should understand that translation data may leave the local project.

low_findings.title: API Key Authentication Guidance
low_findings.description: The documentation references LOCALHERO_API_KEY and login --api-key for non-interactive authentication. This is expected for service authentication, but assistants should avoid printing, committing, or logging real API keys.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Credential-related documentation is explicit and relevant to the CLI. The examples use placeholders and do not show secret exfiltration, so the finding is low severity.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Ruby or shell backtick executions, but the reviewed locations are markdown headings, inline code, or fenced command examples. They are not executable Ruby code inside this skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review confirms these are documentation snippets and markdown formatting. The package contains no Ruby source file or executable wrapper implementing backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts were not supported by semantic review of the cited lines. The cited content describes Localhero.ai, gettext msgid usage, or CLI documentation rather than cryptographic code or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain hashing algorithms, encryption routines, or system inventory commands. The remaining concern is the documented CLI behavior, covered in separate findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Permission for External CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools field permits any subcommand under npx @localheroai/cli *. This broad permission is functional but should be reviewed because future CLI subcommands could expand file, network, or credential behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard permission is directly present in the skill metadata. The risk depends on the external CLI implementation and future command surface.

content.value_statement: Internationalization work can create inconsistent keys, tone, and untranslated UI strings. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Localhero.ai workflows for source copy, glossary use, and translation generation.
actual_capabilities: Reads localhero.json to identify source locale, translation file paths, and project patterns.
Helps add or update source language strings while preserving existing key conventions.
Uses Localhero glossary and settings commands to align terminology, tone, and style.
Runs changed-only translation generation when no Localhero GitHub Action is present.
Directs users to the Localhero.ai web UI for review, glossary, and settings tasks.
limitations: Requires a project configured for Localhero.ai with localhero.json available.
Requires Localhero CLI authentication before translation commands can run.
Only writes source language strings; Localhero.ai handles target translations.
External CLI behavior depends on the installed @localheroai/cli package.

use_cases.title: Add New UI Copy
use_cases.description: Create source locale keys for new interface text while matching existing naming and tone.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Request Translations
use_cases.description: Generate translations for changed strings or rely on the Localhero GitHub Action when configured.

use_cases.title: Align Product Terminology
use_cases.description: Use glossary and project settings to keep user-facing strings consistent across features.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Simple String
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a source locale string for the new save confirmation message. Follow the existing key naming pattern and do not edit target locales.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the dashboard welcome copy for clarity. Check localhero.json, inspect nearby keys, and preserve the project tone.

prompt_templates.title: Use Glossary and Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the Localhero glossary and project settings, then add source strings for the billing upgrade flow using approved terms.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Translation Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: After adding source strings for this feature, check whether a Localhero GitHub Action exists. If not, run changed-only translation generation and summarize the modified files.

output_examples.input: Add copy for a new account deletion confirmation dialog.
output: Identifies the source locale and translation file path from localhero.json.
Adds source keys for the title, body, cancel action, and confirm action.
Leaves target locale files for Localhero.ai translation generation.

output_examples.input: Prepare translations for my current branch.
output: Checks whether the repository uses the Localhero GitHub Action.
Runs changed-only translation generation when manual CLI generation is needed.
Reports which translation files changed and which review steps remain.

output_examples.input: Make the checkout copy match our product terminology.
output: Loads glossary terms and project tone settings from the Localhero CLI.
Rewrites source strings using approved terminology.
Recommends web UI review when translated wording needs human adjustment.

best_practices: Review localhero.json and nearby source strings before adding new keys.
Use changed-only translation generation to keep pull request diffs focused.
Keep API keys in environment variables or secret stores, never in committed files.
anti_patterns: Do not manually edit generated target locale files when Localhero.ai manages them.
Do not invent key naming conventions without checking existing translation files.
Do not paste real Localhero API keys into prompts, logs, or source files.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding assistants manage Localhero.ai source strings, glossary context, project settings, and translation generation workflows.

faq.question: Does it translate text directly inside the assistant?
faq.answer: No. The skill writes source language strings and uses Localhero.ai or its CLI for target translations.

faq.question: Which files does it need?
faq.answer: It expects localhero.json and the translation files configured in that project file.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. Localhero CLI commands require login or LOCALHERO_API_KEY for non-interactive environments.

faq.question: Can it work with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. If a Localhero GitHub Action is configured, the skill tells the user that translations run on the pull request.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should review source copy, generated translation diffs, and any Localhero.ai web UI suggestions for sensitive wording.
